title: Animal and human bites - NHS
text:
lose the wound with stitches, sticky strips or special glue, or leave it open to heal give you antibiotics to stop the wound becoming infected check your risk of getting tetanus and rabies – you'll be given treatment to prevent these serious infections if needed If you've been bitten by a person, you may also be offered a blood test to check for hepatitis B , hepatitis C and HIV .
Page last reviewed: 27 October 2025
Next review due: 27 October 2028
